4-judge ensemble on red_5k_v2 (1500 grit_v2 + 1500 cc3m + 700 vg + 300 oi + 300 pixmo)
Each judged sample shows the 4-judge verdict chips + brief reasons. Categorized by agreement: ALL-AGREE-YES = definite false-reject (Molmo correct, GT too restrictive); ALL-AGREE-NO = real hard example (Molmo wrong); tied = ambiguous.
Judges done: GPT-5=5000/4300, Qwen3-VL-32B=5000/4300, InternVL3-78B=5000/4300, GLM-4.5V=5000/4300 · 3-judge intersection=5000 · 4-judge intersection=5000
datasetnall_mask_wrmaj_mask_wrall_yesmaj_yestiedmaj_noall_no

pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 179 281 2 215 233 3 230 281 4 358 124 5 595 116 6 668 266 7 698 291 8 745 301 9 800 151 10 875 116 11 885 301 12 915 366">Man</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow masks mostly cover tables/chairs and broad regions, not specifically the men; they miss several men and include women and background areas, so annotation is inaccurate for “Man.” Stage 2: Several red dots are on women or non-person areas and not all men are covered; thus the dots do not correctly or consistently indicate men.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask includes multiple people, but many of them are women (e.g., the person in the center with pink shirt, and several seated individuals with long hair). The mask also covers non-human objects like chairs and tables. The phrase is 'Man', so the mask should only cover male individuals. The mask appears to cover at least 7 instances, but many are clearly not men, making the annotation incorrect. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on various people, including several women (e.g., the person with long brown hair in the center, and others with long hair seated at the table). Some dots are also on people who appear to be women or whose gender is ambiguous. The model has placed 12 dots, but many are not on men, indicating incorrect predictions. The model also seems to have missed some visible men (e.g., the man in the suit standing in the back center is not marked). Thus, the red dots do not correctly point to 'Man'.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow region covers both men and women, which is incorrect for the phrase 'Man'. It is over-annotated. Stage 2: The red dots point at both men and women, indicating incorrect identification for the phrase 'Man'.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation includes non-man elements (e.g., women, chairs) and misses visible men, so it's over-annotated and inaccurate. Stage 2: Many red dots point to women or non-human objects, so they don't correctly identify men.

Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow GT highlights a tall vertical strip at the right edge, not the furniture covers themselves. It misses all actual one-piece polyester furniture covers shown on the sofas and chairs. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on various sofas and chairs across colors, which are the furniture covers. However, there are many instances and the dot count/placement does not consistently correspond to all covers shown; several pieces are missing and some dots may be on cushions/areas inconsistently. Therefore the dots do not accurately or comprehensively indicate the phrase.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask highlights only a single armchair cover on the right side of the middle row, but the phrase refers to 'this one-piece, polyester furniture cover' — which, given the image, is a product type shown in multiple instances (three sets of sofa/loveseat/armchair covers in different colors). The GT mask incorrectly isolates just one instance, failing to represent the full set or the general product type. It is under-annotated and misleading. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on multiple furniture covers across all three color sets (red, blue, beige), totaling 9 points. However, the phrase refers to 'this one-piece, polyester furniture cover' — implying a singular instance or a specific one. The model is treating all visible covers as valid, which is incorrect if the phrase is meant to refer to a single item. Since the phrase is singular and the model points to 9 instances, it over-identifies and misses the intended singular focus. Thus, the red dots do not correctly po
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow region is over-annotated, covering multiple furniture covers instead of just one. Stage 2: The red dots correctly point to the furniture covers, matching the phrase.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is on the wrong object (a chair with a different cover style) and does not match the target phrase's one-piece polyester covers. Stage 2: All 9 red dots correctly point to one-piece polyester furniture covers across the image, matching the visible instances.

GPT-5: The phrase is 'White tubes'. The yellow mask highlights a pale yellow tube on a round container, not the white tubes in the scene, so the GT annotation is incorrect. The red dots are on three tubes: one on the yellow tube and two on the white tubes. Since one dot points to a non-white tube, the red predictions are not all correct for 'White tubes'.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask highlights a yellow tube, not a white tube. The phrase is 'White tubes', and the mask is clearly on a non-white object, so it is incorrect. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on two white tubes (one hexagonal, one cylindrical) and one yellow tube. Since the yellow tube is not a 'white tube', at least one red dot is incorrectly placed. Therefore, the model's predictions are not fully correct.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is on a yellow tube, not a white tube, so it is incorrect. Stage 2: The red dots point at white tubes, matching the phrase.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation marks a single yellow tube, but the phrase 'White tubes' refers to the three white tubes in the image. The yellow mask is on the wrong object (yellow instead of white) and misses the actual white tube instances, so 'mask_wrong'. Stage 2: The three red dots are positioned on the three white tubes, which are instances of the phrase 'White tubes'. All red dots point at valid instances, and the count (3) matches the visible white tubes, so 'yes'.

The phrase 'the beamed ceilings' refers to ceiling structures with beams, which are not present or marked in the yellow region. Thus, the mask is incorrect. Stage 2: The two red dots are placed on the top edge of the brick fireplace and the top of the wooden door frame — neither of which are beamed ceilings. The model has misidentified the target, placing points on unrelated architectural elements. Therefore, the red dots do not correctly point to 'the beamed ceilings'.

The phrase refers to a long, prominent text block that spans the top and sides of the image, which is not captured by the yellow mask. Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers a large portion of the building's corner, including windows, but it also includes non-window areas such as the ground floor storefront, signage, and parts of the sidewalk. The phrase 'several windows on building' specifically refers to windows, not the entire facade. The mask is over-annotated and includes irrelevant regions, making it incorrect. Stage 2: The three red dots are placed directly on distinct windows on the upper floor of the building, which are clearly visible and match the phrase. The model correctly identified windows, and the count of 3 is reasonable given the visible windows. Thus, the red dots are accurate.

GLM: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers the entire building and sidewalk, which is over-annotated for 'several windows on building'—it should only mark the windows, not the whole structure. Stage 2: The single red dot is placed on the forehead of the young girl in the image, who appears to have a serious or defiant expression, fitting the description of a 'defiant child' in a homeschool context. It does not accurately delineate the cars — many yellow points are clearly not on any car, and the mask fails to cover all visible cars. This is over-annotation and misalignment with the phrase 'cars'. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on visible cars throughout the parking lot. They are distributed across multiple vehicles, including sedans, SUVs, and trucks, and there are approximately 41 dots matching the number of visible cars. No red dot appears to be on a non-car object. Thus, the model correctly identifies and points to cars, even if the GT mask is flawed.

GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is scattered and does not accurately cover the cars in the parking lot, appearing to be misaligned or incorrectly placed. There are no red dots on non-rock areas (e.g., gaps or background), so the model’s predictions are accurate and comprehensive.

GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow marks highlight multiple small calendars on the right and some dots around the large calendar header, not the individual day cells within January; it misses most of the January day squares and includes other months—over/incorrectly annotated. Stage 2: The red dots are scattered over various dates in the January grid but do not cover all days; they represent a random subset rather than all instances of ‘day in January,’ so many instances are missed.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers the entire January calendar, including the header and the grid, but it does not accurately isolate individual 'days' — it marks the whole calendar as one region. The phrase 'day in january' implies individual date cells (e.g., 1st, 2nd, ..., 31st), not the entire calendar. The mask is over-annotated and does not correspond to the 31 individual days. Hence, mask_wrong. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on individual date cells within the January calendar (e.g., 1, 2, 3, ..., 31), and there are 24 dots. While 24 is less than 31, the dots are correctly placed on actual days in January, and there are no dots on non-days or wrong objects. The model has not pointed to wrong objects, and the majority of visible days are covered. Thus, molmo_verdict is 'yes' — the dots point to correct instances, even if not all are covered.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow annotation accurately covers all days in January, matching the 31 days visible. Stage 2: The red dots point to days in January, and the count of red dots roughly matches the number of days.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow translucent area accurately covers the January calendar days (31 instances) without extending to unrelated objects, matching the phrase "day in january". Stage 2: All 24 red dots point at valid days in January, and while not all 31 days are marked, the count roughly matches visible instances (model didn't miss most).

The model missed the broader, more colorful architectural and decorative elements visible in the room.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow regions accurately cover the colorful interiors, including the walls and ceiling, without extending onto unrelated objects. Stage 2: The red dot does not point to any instance of the colorful interiors; it is located on a plain area, missing the colorful elements.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotations accurately cover colorful interior elements (arches, wall decorations, cushions) without extending to unrelated areas, and the count of 5 matches visible instances. Stage 2: The single red dot points at a plain wall section, not a colorful interior element, so it fails to correctly identify an instance of 'Colorful interiors'.
